context: You are in hot Spain and you are not just browsing anymore. You ask for the clothes you want, ask about fabric, and solve size problems until the fit is right.
explanation: Start with three high-power chunks: "busco" (I am looking for), "necesito" (I need), and "tiene" (do you have). With only these, you can drive the interaction instead of waiting for the clerk.

examples.en: I am looking for a linen shirt
examples.note: Use busco to lead with intent.

examples.en: I need trousers for hot weather

examples.note: Use esta/esa to point at a specific item.
Close the conversation confidently when it fits.
